tests.test_body:
define void @ashr_out_of_range(ptr %A) {
; CHECK-LABEL: @ashr_out_of_range(
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[L:%.*]] = load i177, ptr [[A:%.*]], align 4
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP1:%.*]] = icmp eq i177 [[L]], -1
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP2:%.*]] = select i1 [[TMP1]], i64 -1, i64 -2
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[G11:%.*]] = getelementptr i177, ptr [[A]], i64 [[TMP2]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[L7:%.*]] = load i177, ptr [[G11]], align 4
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[L7_FROZEN:%.*]] = freeze i177 [[L7]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[C171:%.*]] = icmp slt i177 [[L7_FROZEN]], 0
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[C17:%.*]] = and i1 [[TMP1]], [[C171]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP3:%.*]] = sext i1 [[C17]] to i64
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[G62:%.*]] = getelementptr i177, ptr [[G11]], i64 [[TMP3]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[TMP4:%.*]] = icmp eq i177 [[L7_FROZEN]], -1
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[B28:%.*]] = select i1 [[TMP4]], i177 0, i177 [[L7_FROZEN]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    store i177 [[B28]], ptr [[G62]], align 4
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret void
  %L = load i177, ptr %A, align 4
  %B5 = udiv i177 %L, -1
  %B4 = add i177 %B5, -1
  %B2 = add i177 %B4, -1
  %G11 = getelementptr i177, ptr %A, i177 %B2
  %L7 = load i177, ptr %G11, align 4
  %B6 = mul i177 %B5, %B2
  %B24 = ashr i177 %L7, %B6
  %B36 = and i177 %L7, %B4
  %C17 = icmp sgt i177 %B36, %B24
  %G62 = getelementptr i177, ptr %G11, i1 %C17
  %B28 = urem i177 %B24, %B6
  store i177 %B28, ptr %G62, align 4
  ret void
}
tests.source_program:
define void @ashr_out_of_range(ptr %A) {
  %L = load i177, ptr %A, align 4
  %B5 = udiv i177 %L, -1
  %B4 = add i177 %B5, -1
  %B2 = add i177 %B4, -1
  %G11 = getelementptr i177, ptr %A, i177 %B2
  %L7 = load i177, ptr %G11, align 4
  %B6 = mul i177 %B5, %B2
  %B24 = ashr i177 %L7, %B6
  %B36 = and i177 %L7, %B4
  %C17 = icmp sgt i177 %B36, %B24
  %G62 = getelementptr i177, ptr %G11, i1 %C17
  %B28 = urem i177 %B24, %B6
  store i177 %B28, ptr %G62, align 4
  ret void
}

issue.title: [InstCombine] Miscompilation on vector operations with undef and poison elements
issue.body:
The following test case is miscompiled by InstCombine:

```ll
define <2 x i32> @src(<2 x i1> %x, <2 x i32> %y) {
%0:
  %sext.i1 = sext <2 x i1> %x to <2 x i32>
  %r = urem <2 x i32> %y, %sext.i1
  ret <2 x i32> %r
}
=>
define <2 x i32> @tgt(<2 x i1> %x, <2 x i32> %y) {
%0:
  %1 = icmp eq <2 x i32> %y, { 4294967295, 4294967295 }
  %r = select <2 x i1> %1, <2 x i32> { 0, 0 }, <2 x i32> %y
  ret <2 x i32> %r
}
Transformation doesn't verify!

ERROR: Value mismatch
```

Here is the alive2 output that produces an example input that causes a value mismatch: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/JX-TLu
And here is the original IR file that produced the miscompilation:

```ll
define <2 x i32> @zext_bool_urem_divisor_vec(<2 x i1> %x, <2 x i32> %y) {
  %ext = zext <2 x i1> %x to <2 x i32>
  %sext.i = sext <2 x i1> %x to <2 x i32>
  %sext.i1 = sext <2 x i1> %x to <2 x i32>
  %r.i2 = xor <2 x i32> <i32 42, i32 -7>, %sext.i1
  %r.i = xor <2 x i32> <i32 42, i32 -7>, %r.i2
  %r = urem <2 x i32> %y, %r.i
  ret <2 x i32> %r
}
```
issue.author: ericliuu

comments.author: floatshadow
comments.body:
The replacement happens here https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/fe3ed6550ef277f5c47a9ccdbdd466fa225f4a99/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineMulDivRem.cpp#L1917-L1925


I'm a bit confused. Alive2 tells me when
```ll
<2 x i32> %y = < undef, poison >
```
we have
```ll
Target:
<2 x i1> %1 = < #x0 (0), poison >
<2 x i32> %r = < #xffffffff (4294967295, -1), poison >
```
